questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt: The '2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development', adopted by all United Nations Member States in 2015, succeeded which of the following frameworks?
questions.explanation: The Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) were adopted in 2015 as a successor to the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs), expanding the scope to 17 goals and 169 targets.
questions.takeaway: The SDGs are the direct successor to the MDGs, transitioning from 8 goals to a more comprehensive set of 17 goals.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: SDGs were adopted in 2015 (17 goals, 169 targets), succeeding the Millennium Development Goals (MDGs).

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
Consider the following pairs of Reports and their Publishing Organizations:

1. SDG India Index : NITI Aayog
2. National Indicator Framework Progress Report : MoSPI
3. Sustainable Development Report (SDR) : UN DESA

Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Pair 1 is correctly matched: NITI Aayog releases the SDG India Index. Pair 2 is correctly matched: MoSPI releases the National Indicator Framework Progress Report. Pair 3 is incorrectly matched: The SDR is released by the Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN), whereas UN DESA prepares the Sustainable Development Goals Report (SDGR).
questions.takeaway: Be careful with the naming: 'Sustainable Development Report' (SDR) is SDSN, while 'Sustainable Development Goals Report' (SDGR) is UN DESA.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Sustainable Development Report (SDR) 2025 Released by: SDG Transformation Centre, under Sustainable Development Solutions Network (SDSN). Sustainable Development Goals Report (SDGR) 2025 Prepared by: UN DESA. SDG India Index: Released by: NITI Aayog. MoSPI launched the National Indicator Framework (NIF) Progress Report 2025.
